The classification header has already been emitted. Do NOT emit it again. This skill handles **execution only** for STANDARD and STRICT tier tasks.\n>\n> If for any reason no classification header was emitted before this skill was invoked, emit one now using ONLY the tier values STRICT, STANDARD, LIGHT, or EXEMPT (no other values are valid).\n\n# /sc:task-unified - Unified Task Execution with Compliance\n\n## Purpose\n\nUnified command that merges orchestration capabilities with MCP compliance enforcement. Automatically classifies tasks into compliance tiers and enforces appropriate verification.\n\n**Key Benefits**:\n- Single command replaces confusion between `sc:task` and `sc:task-mcp`\n- Automatic tier classification with confidence scoring\n- Appropriate verification for each task type\n- Prevents over-engineering for trivial changes\n- Ensures safety for critical changes\n\n## Triggers\n\nUse `/sc:task` when:\n- Task involves code modifications with downstream impacts\n- Complexity score >0.6 with code modifications\n- Multi-file scope (>2 estimated affected files)\n- Security domain paths detected (auth/, security/, crypto/)\n- Refactoring or system-wide changes requested\n\n**Auto-Suggest Keywords**:\n- High confidence: \"implement feature\", \"refactor system\", \"fix security\", \"add authentication\", \"update database schema\"\n- Moderate confidence: \"add new\", \"create component\", \"update service\", \"modify API\"\n\n## Usage\n\n```bash\n/sc:task [description]                           # Auto-detect all dimensions\n/sc:task [description] --compliance strict       # Force STRICT tier\n/sc:task [description] --compliance light        # Force LIGHT tier\n/sc:task [description] --skip-compliance         # Bypass compliance (escape hatch)\n/sc:task [description] --verify auto             # Auto-select verification\n```\n\n## Behavioral Flow\n\n### 0. Classification (Already Performed)\n\nClassification was handled by the `/sc:task` command before this skill was invoked. The tier has been determined and the classification header has been emitted. Proceed directly to execution based on the classified tier.\n\n**Reference** — the tier keyword tables (for context only, do not re-classify):\n- **STRICT**: security, authentication, database, migration, refactor, breaking change, encrypt, token, session, oauth\n- **EXEMPT**: explain, search, commit, push, plan, discuss, brainstorm\n- **LIGHT**: typo, comment, whitespace, lint, docstring, formatting, minor\n- **STANDARD**: implement, add, create, update, fix, build, modify, change (default)\n\n### 2. Confidence Display (Human-Readable)\n\nAfter the mandatory classification header, show a human-readable summary:\n\n```\n**Tier: STANDARD** [████████░░] 80%\n\nClassified as STANDARD:\n- Keywords matched: add, implement\n- Confidence score: 0.78\n- Considered alternatives: STRICT (0.35)\n```\n\nIf confidence <70%, add prompt: \"⚠️ Low confidence. Override with: `--compliance [strict|standard|light|exempt]`\"\n\n**Note**: The machine-readable header (Section 0) is for telemetry/A/B testing. This human-readable display is for user understanding.\n\n### 3. Execution Phase\n\nExecute task according to tier requirements:\n\n**STRICT Execution**:\n1. Activate project (mcp__serena__activate_project)\n2. Verify git working directory clean (git status)\n3. Load codebase context (codebase-retrieval)\n4. Check relevant memories (list_memories -> read_memory)\n5. Identify all affected files and test files\n6. Make changes with full checklist\n7. Identify all files that import changed code\n8. Update all affected files\n9. Spawn verification agent (quality-engineer)\n10. Run comprehensive tests: `pytest [path] -v`\n11. Answer adversarial questions\n\n**STANDARD Execution**:\n1. Load context via codebase-retrieval\n2. Search downstream impacts (find_referencing_symbols OR grep)\n3. Make changes\n4. Run affected tests OR document manual verification\n5. Verify basic functionality\n\n**LIGHT Execution**:\n1. Quick scope check (files/lines within bounds)\n2. Make changes\n3. Quick sanity check (syntax valid, no obvious errors)\n4. Proceed with judgment\n\n**EXEMPT Execution**:\n1. Execute immediately\n2. No verification overhead\n\n### 4. Verification Phase\n\nRoute to appropriate verification based on tier and paths:\n\n| Compliance Tier | Verification Method | Token Cost | Timeout |\n|-----------------|---------------------|------------|---------|\n| STRICT | Sub-agent (quality-engineer) | 3-5K | 60s |\n| STANDARD | Direct test execution | 300-500 | 30s |\n| LIGHT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n| EXEMPT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n\n**Critical Path Override**: Paths matching `auth/`, `security/`, `crypto/`, `models/`, `migrations/` always trigger CRITICAL verification regardless of compliance tier.\n\n**Trivial Path Override**: Paths matching `*.md`, `docs/`, `*test*.py` may skip verification.\n\n### 5. Feedback Collection\n\nAfter completion, collect implicit feedback:\n- Track if user overrode tier (implicit classification feedback)\n- Note smooth completion vs errors (quality signal)\n- Store for calibration learning\n\n## MCP Integration\n\n**Required Servers by Tier**:\n- STRICT: Sequential, Serena (fallback not allowed)\n- STANDARD: Sequential, Context7 (fallback allowed)\n- LIGHT: None required (fallback allowed)\n- EXEMPT: None required\n\n**Circuit Breaker Behavior**:\n- If required servers unavailable for STRICT tier, block task execution\n- For other tiers, use fallbacks with noted limitations\n\n## Tool Coordination\n\n**Planning Phase**:\n1. TodoWrite: Create task breakdown\n2. codebase-retrieval: Load context\n3. list_memories / read_memory: Check project state\n\n**Execution Phase**:\n1. Edit/MultiEdit/Write: Make changes\n2. Grep/Glob: Find references\n3. find_referencing_symbols: Trace dependencies\n\n**Verification Phase**:\n1. Task (quality-engineer): Spawn verification agent (STRICT only)\n2. Bash: Run tests directly (STANDARD)\n3. think_about_task_adherence: Reflect on completeness\n\n**Completion Phase**:\n1. write_memory: Save session state\n2. think_about_whether_you_are_done: Final check\n\n## Examples\n\n### STRICT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"implement user authentication with JWT\"\n\n-> Classified as STRICT (security domain, authentication keyword)\n-> Full 6-category checklist enforced\n-> Verification agent spawned\n-> Adversarial questions answered\n```\n\n### STANDARD Task\n```\n/sc:task \"add pagination to user list\"\n\n-> Classified as STANDARD (add keyword, typical feature)\n-> Context loaded before editing\n-> Downstream impacts checked\n-> Direct test execution\n```\n\n### LIGHT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"fix typo in README\"\n\n-> Classified as LIGHT (trivial keyword, documentation path)\n-> Quick sanity check only\n-> No verification delay\n```\n\n### EXEMPT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"explain how the auth flow works\"\n\n-> Classified as EXEMPT (explain pattern detected)\n-> Immediate execution\n-> No compliance overhead\n```\n\n### Override Example\n```\n/sc:task \"update config file\" --compliance strict\n\n-> User override to STRICT regardless of classification\n-> Full verification enforced\n```\n\n## Boundaries\n\n**Will:**\n- Classify tasks into appropriate compliance tiers\n- Enforce tier-appropriate verification requirements\n- Provide confidence scoring with rationale\n- Track feedback for continuous calibration\n- Support user overrides with justification\n\n**Will Not:**\n- Skip safety-critical verification for STRICT tasks\n- Apply STRICT overhead to genuinely trivial changes\n- Override user's explicit compliance choice\n- Proceed with <70% confidence without user confirmation\n\n## Success Criteria\n\n| Metric | Target | Measurement |\n|--------|--------|-------------|\n| Tier classification accuracy | >=80% | User feedback on appropriateness |\n| User confusion rate | <10% | \"Which command?\" questions eliminated |\n| Skip rate (--skip-compliance) | <12% | Override tracking |\n| Regression prevention | >=85% | Post-verification bug detection |\n| STRICT tier overhead | <25% | Execution telemetry |\n\n## Configuration References\n\n- Keywords: `config/tier-keywords.yaml`\n- Verification routing: `config/verification-routing.yaml`\n- Acceptance criteria: `config/tier-acceptance-criteria.yaml`\n- Circuit breakers: See MCP.md\n- Routing logic: See ORCHESTRATOR.md\n\n\nARGUMENTS: Execute all tasks in Strategy 1: parse index file\n    index_text = index_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    for match in PHASE_FILE_PATTERN.finditer(index_text):\n        num = int(next(g for g in match.groups() if g is not None))\n        filename = match.group(0)\n        filepath = index_dir / filename\n        if filepath.exists() and num not in phases:\n            phases[num] = Phase(number=num, file=filepath)\n\n    # Strip markdown heading and phase number prefix\n                name = re.sub(r\"^#\\s+(?:Phase\\s+\\d+\\s*[-:—]\\s*)?\", \"\", line)\n                return name[:50]  # truncate\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n    return \"\"\n\n\ndef validate_phases(\n    phases: list[Phase],\n    start: int,\n    end: int,\n) -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Validate phase files exist and check for gaps.\n\n    Returns separated buckets with keys: errors, warnings.\n    \"\"\"\n    errors: list[str] = []\n    warnings: list[str] = []\n    active = [p for p in phases if start <= p.number <= end]\n\n    # Check files exist\n    for p in active:\n        if not p.file.exists():\n            errors.append(f\"ERROR: Phase {p.number} file missing: {p.file}\")\n\n    # Check for gaps\n    numbers = [p.number for p in active]\n    for i in range(1, len(numbers)):\n        if numbers[i] != numbers[i - 1] + 1:\n            warnings.append(\n                f\"WARN: Gap in sequence: Phase {numbers[i-1]} -> Phase {numbers[i]}\"\n            )\n\n    return {\"errors\": errors, \"warnings\": warnings}\n\n\ndef load_sprint_config(\n    index_path: Path,\n    start_phase: int = 1,\n    end_phase: int = 0,\n    max_turns: int = 50,\n    model: str = \"\",\n    dry_run: bool = False,\n    permission_flag: str = \"--dangerously-skip-permissions\",\n    debug: bool = False,\n    stall_timeout: int = 0,\n    stall_action: str = \"warn\",\n) -> SprintConfig:\n    \"\"\"Load and validate a complete sprint configuration.\"\"\"\n    index_path = Path(index_path).resolve()\n\n    if not index_path.exists():\n        raise click.ClickException(f\"Index file not found: {index_path}\")\n\n    phases = discover_phases(index_path)\n    if not phases:\n        raise click.ClickException(\n            \"No phase files discovered. Expected canonical filenames like: \"\n            \"phase-1-tasklist.md, p1-tasklist.md, phase_1_tasklist.md, tasklist-p1.md\"\n        )\n\n    # Enrich phases with names\n    for phase in phases:\n        phase.name = _extract_phase_name(phase.file)\n\n    # Auto-detect end phase\n    if end_phase == 0:\n        end_phase = max(p.number for p in phases)\n\n    # Validate\n    validation = validate_phases(phases, start_phase, end_phase)\n    errors = validation[\"errors\"]\n    warnings = validation[\"warnings\"]\n    if errors:\n        for e in errors:\n            click.echo(e, err=True)\n        raise click.ClickException(f\"{len(errors)} phase file(s) missing.\")\n\n    for w in warnings:\n        click.echo(w, err=True)\n\n    config = SprintConfig(\n        index_path=index_path,\n        release_dir=index_path.parent,\n        phases=phases,\n        start_phase=start_phase,\n        end_phase=end_phase,\n        max_turns=max_turns,\n        model=model,\n        dry_run=dry_run,\n        permission_flag=permission_flag,\n        debug=debug,\n        stall_timeout=stall_timeout,\n        stall_action=stall_action,\n    )\n\n    # Validate that the requested range yields at least one active phase\n    if not config.active_phases:\n        available = [p.number for p in phases]\n        raise click.ClickException(\n            f\"No phases in range [{start_phase}, {end_phase}]. \"\n            f\"Available phase numbers: {available}\"\n        )\n\n    return config\n","numLines":172,"startLine":1,"totalLines":172}}}

Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n   333→    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n   334→    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n   335→    4. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n   349→        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n   350→        upper = content.upper()\n   351→        has_continue = message if budget is insufficient.\n\n    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n    if the budget is too low to launch a subprocess.\n    \"\"\"\n    if ledger is None:\n        return None\n    if ledger.can_launch():\n        return None\n    return (\n        f\"Budget exhausted: {ledger.available()} turns remaining, \"\n        f\"minimum {ledger.minimum_allocation} required for launch\"\n    )\n\n\ndef execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n\n    For each active phase:\n    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n    2. Start output monitor thread\n    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n    5. Record PhaseResult\n    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n        raise SystemExit(\n            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n        )\n\n    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n    signal_handler.install()\n\n    setup_debug_logger(config)\n    import logging as _logging\n    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n\n    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n                    _timed_out = True\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n\n                ms = monitor.state\n                _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n\n                debug_log(\n                    _dbg,\n                    \"poll_tick\",\n                    phase=phase.number,\n                    pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    poll_result=\"running\",\n                    elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n                    output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n                    growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n                    stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                    stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n                )\n\n                # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n                if (\n                    config.stall_timeout > 0\n                    and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n                    and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n                    and not _stall_acted\n                ):\n                    _stall_acted = True\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"watchdog_triggered\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        action=config.stall_action,\n                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    )\n                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n                        _timed_out = True\n                        proc_manager.terminate()\n                        break\n                    else:\n                        # warn action: log and continue\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n\n                # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n                if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n                    _stall_acted = False\n\n                # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n                # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n                try:\n                    tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n                except Exception as _tui_exc:\n                    import sys\n                    print(f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n\n            # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n            # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n            raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n            if _timed_out:\n                exit_code = 124\n            else:\n                exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n            monitor.stop()\n            finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"PHASE_END\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n            # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n            # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n            # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                logger.write_phase_interrupt(phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code)\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Determine phase status\n            status = _determine_phase_status(\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                result_file=config.result_file(phase),\n                output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n            )\n\n            # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n            error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n            error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n\n            phase_result = PhaseResult(\n                phase=phase,\n                status=status,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n                error_bytes=error_bytes,\n                last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n                files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n            )\n            sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"phase_complete\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                status=status.value,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # Log and notify\n            logger.write_phase_result(phase_result)\n            notify_phase_complete(phase_result)\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, None)\n\n            # Decide: continue or halt?\n            if status.is_failure:\n                # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n                try:\n                    collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n                    bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n                    classifier = FailureClassifier()\n                    bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n                    reporter = ReportGenerator()\n                    diag_path = config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n                    reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"diagnostic_report\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        category=bundle.category.value,\n                        path=str(diag_path),\n                    )\n                except Exception as _diag_exc:\n                    debug_log(_dbg, \"diagnostic_error\", phase=phase.number, error=str(_diag_exc))\n\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n                sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n                break\n\n        # Sprint finished\n        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n            # Verify all phases actually passed\n            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n\n        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n\n    finally:\n        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n        try:\n            monitor.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        if proc_manager is not None:\n            try:\n                proc_manager.terminate()\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n        try:\n            tui.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        try:\n            signal_handler.uninstall()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n    try:\n        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n    except OSError:\n        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n\n    if _exitcode != 0:\n        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n\n\ndef _determine_phase_status(\n    exit_code: int,\n    result_file: Path,\n    output_file: Path,\n) -> PhaseStatus:\n    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n\n    Priority:\n    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n    \"\"\"\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    if result_file.exists():\n        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n        upper = content.upper()\n        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n        if has_halt:\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        if has_continue:\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n","numLines":375,"startLine":1,"totalLines":375}}}

284→@dataclass\n   285→class TurnLedger:\n   286→    \"\"\"Economic model for subprocess turn budget tracking.\n   287→\n   288→    Tracks budget allocation, consumption, and reimbursement for for sprint debug logging.\n\n    Captures phase number and config output/error paths so the hook\n    can log spawn and files_opened events with full context.\n    \"\"\"\n    output_file = config.output_file(phase)\n    error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n    phase_number = phase.number\n\n    def on_spawn(pid: int) -> None:\n        debug_log(\n            _dbg,\n \"\"\"Factory returning an on_signal closure for sprint debug logging.\n\n    Logs signal_sent events with the signal name and process id.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def on_signal(pid: int, signal_name: str) -> None:\n        debug_log(_dbg, \"signal_sent\", signal=signal_name, pid=pid)\n\n    return on_signal\n\n\ndef _make_exit_hook(phase: Phase, config: SprintConfig):\n    \"\"\"Factory returning an on_exit closure for sprint debug logging.\n\n    Logs exit events with pid, return code, and timeout detection.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def on_exit(pid: int, returncode: int | None) -> None:\n        debug_log(\n            _dbg,\n            \"exit\",\n            pid=pid,\n            code=returncode,\n            was_timeout=(returncode == 124),\n        )\n\n    return on_exit\n\n\nclass ClaudeProcess(_PipelineClaudeProcess):\n    \"\"\"Sprint-specific claude process extending the pipeline base.\n\n    Defines only __init__ (with hook wiring) and build_prompt().\n    All subprocess lifecycle (start, wait, terminate) is inherited\n    from the pipeline base class; sprint debug logging is injected\n    via lifecycle hook factories.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, config: permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n            timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n            output_format=\"stream-json\",\n            on_spawn=_make_spawn_hook(phase, config),\n            on_signal=_make_signal_hook(phase, config),\n            on_exit=_make_exit_hook(phase, config),\n        )\n\n    def build_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the /sc:task-unified prompt for this phase.\"\"\"\n        pn = self.phase.number\n        result_file = self.config.result_file(self.phase)\n        phase_file = self.phase.file\n\n        return (\n            f\"/sc:task-unified Execute all tasks in @{phase_file} \"\n            f\"--compliance strict --strategy systematic\\n\"\n            f\"\\n\"\n            f\"## Execution Rules\\n\"\n            f\"- EXEMPT tier tasks: skip formal verification\\n\"\n            f\"- If a STRICT-tier task fails, STOP and report -- \"\n            f\"do not continue to next task\\n\"\n            f\"- For all other Strategy 1: parse index file\n    index_text = index_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    for match in PHASE_FILE_PATTERN.finditer(index_text):\n        num = int(next(g for g in match.groups() if g is not None))\n        filename = match.group(0)\n        filepath = index_dir / filename\n        if filepath.exists() and num not in phases:\n            phases[num] = Phase(number=num, file=filepath)\n\n    # Strategy 2: scan directory if nothing found\n    if not phases:\n        for f in sorted(index_dir.iterdir()):\n            if f.name == index_name or not f.is_file():\n                continue\n            m = PHASE_FILE_PATTERN.search(f.name)\n            if m:\n                num = int(next(g for g in m.groups() if g is not None))\n                if num not in phases:\n                    phases[num] = Phase(number=num, file=f)\n\n    return [phases[k] for k in sorted(phases)]\n\n\ndef _extract_phase_name(phase_file: Path) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Try to extract a phase name from the first heading.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        for line in phase_file.open():\n            line = line.strip()\n            if line.startswith(\"# \"):\n                # Check files exist\n    for p in active:\n        if not p.file.exists():\n            errors.append(f\"ERROR: Phase {p.number} file missing: {p.file}\")\n\n    # Check for gaps\n    numbers = [p.number for p in active]\n    for i in range(1, len(numbers)):\n        if numbers[i] != numbers[i - 1] + 1:\n            warnings.append(\n                f\"WARN: Gap in sequence: Phase {numbers[i-1]} -> Phase {numbers[i]}\"\n            )\n\n    return {\"errors\": errors, \"warnings\": warnings}\n\n\ndef load_sprint_config(\n    index_path: Path,\n    start_phase: int = 1,\n    end_phase: int = 0,\n    max_turns: int = 50,\n    model: str = \"\",\n    dry_run: bool = False,\n    permission_flag: str = \"--dangerously-skip-permissions\",\n    debug: bool = False,\n    stall_timeout: int = 0,\n    stall_action: str = \"warn\",\n) -> SprintConfig:\n    \"\"\"Load and validate a complete sprint configuration.\"\"\"\n    index_path = Path(index_path).resolve()\n\n    if not index_path.exists():\n        raise click.ClickException(f\"Index file not found: {index_path}\")\n\n    phases = discover_phases(index_path)\n    if not phases:\n        raise click.ClickException(\n            \"No phase files discovered. Expected canonical filenames like: \"\n            \"phase-1-tasklist.md, p1-tasklist.md, phase_1_tasklist.md, tasklist-p1.md\"\n        )\n\n    # Enrich phases with names\n    for phase in phases:\n        phase.name = _extract_phase_name(phase.file)\n\n    # Auto-detect end phase\n    if end_phase == 0:\n        end_phase = max(p.number for p in phases)\n\n    # Validate\n    validation = validate_phases(phases, start_phase, end_phase)\n    errors = validation[\"errors\"]\n    warnings = validation[\"warnings\"]\n    if errors:\n        for e in errors:\n            click.echo(e, err=True)\n        raise click.ClickException(f\"{len(errors)} phase file(s) missing.\")\n\n    for w in warnings:\n        click.echo(w, err=True)\n\n    config = SprintConfig(\n        index_path=index_path,\n        release_dir=index_path.parent,\n        phases=phases,\n        start_phase=start_phase,\n        end_phase=end_phase,\n        max_turns=max_turns,\n        model=model,\n        dry_run=dry_run,\n        permission_flag=permission_flag,\n        debug=debug,\n        stall_timeout=stall_timeout,\n        stall_action=stall_action,\n    )\n\n    # Validate that the requested range yields at least one active phase\n    if not config.active_phases:\n        available = [p.number for p in phases]\n        raise click.ClickException(\n            f\"No phases in range [{start_phase}, {end_phase}]. \"\n            f\"Available phase numbers: {available}\"\n        )\n\n    return config\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":7,"oldLines":7,"newStart":7,"newLines":7,"lines":[" "," import click"," ","-from .models import Phase, SprintConfig","+from .models import Phase, SprintConfig, TaskEntry"," "," # Canonical phase filename conventions (case-insensitive):"," # 1) phase-1-tasklist.md"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

references.\n    Strategy 2: scan the directory for files matching the pattern.\n    Deduplicates by phase number, sorts ascending.\n    \"\"\"\n    index_dir = index_path.parent\n    index_name = index_path.name\n    phases: dict[int, Phase] = {}\n\n    # Strategy 1: parse index file\n    index_text = index_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    for match in PHASE_FILE_PATTERN.finditer(index_text):\n        num = int(next(g for g in match.groups() if g is not None))\n        filename = match.group(0)\n        filepath = index_dir / filename\n        if filepath.exists() and num not in phases:\n            phases[num] = Phase(number=num, file=filepath)\n\n    # Strategy 2: scan directory if nothing found\n    if not phases:\n        for f in return {\"errors\": errors, \"warnings\": warnings}\n\n\ndef load_sprint_config(\n    index_path: Path,\n    start_phase: int = 1,\n    end_phase: int = 0,\n    max_turns: int = 50,\n    model: str = Malformed input (missing headings,\n    invalid IDs, empty content) is handled gracefully — invalid blocks\n    are skipped with a logged warning.\n\n    Args:\n        content: Raw markdown text of a phase tasklist file.\n\n    Returns:\n        Ordered list of :class:`TaskEntry` instances.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not content or not content.strip():\n        return []\n\n    headings = list(_TASK_HEADING_RE.finditer(content))\n    if not headings:\n        _logger.warning(\"No task headings (### T<PP>.<TT>) found in tasklist content\")\n        return []\n\n    tasks: list[TaskEntry] = []\n    for i, match in enumerate(headings):\n        task_id = match.group(1)\n        title = match.group(2).strip()\n\n        # Extract the block body between this heading and the next (or EOF)\n        block_start = match.end()\n        block_end = headings[i + 1].start() if i + 1 < len(headings) else len(content)\n        block = content[block_start:block_end]\n\n        # Extract dependencies\n        dependencies: list[str] = []\n        dep_match = _DEPENDENCY_RE.search(block)\n        if dep_match:\n            dep_text = dep_match.group(1).strip()\n            if dep_text.lower() not in (\"none\", \"\"):\n                dependencies = _TASK_ID_REF_RE.findall(dep_text)\n\n        # Build description from the deliverables section if present\n        description = \"\"\n        for line in block.splitlines():\n            stripped = line.strip()\n            if stripped.startswith(\"**Deliverables:**\"):\n                # Grab the next non-empty line(s) after \"Deliverables:\"\n                idx = block.index(stripped) + len(stripped)\n                rest = block[idx:].strip()\n                desc_lines = []\n                for dline in rest.splitlines():\n                    dline = dline.strip()\n                    if not dline or dline.startswith(\"**\"):\n                        break\n                    # Strip leading \"- \" from markdown list items\n                    if dline.startswith(\"- \"):\n                        dline = dline[2:]\n                    desc_lines.append(dline)\n                description = \" \".join(desc_lines)\n                break\n\n        tasks.append(\n            TaskEntry(\n                task_id=task_id,\n                title=title,\n                description=description,\n                dependencies=dependencies,\n            )\n        )\n\n    return tasks\n\n\ndef parse_tasklist_file(path: Path) -> list[TaskEntry]:\n    \"\"\"Read a phase tasklist file and parse it into a task inventory.\n\n    Args:\n        path: Path to the phase tasklist markdown file.\n\n    Returns:\n        Ordered list of :class:`TaskEntry` instances.\n\n    Raises:\n        FileNotFoundError: If the path does not exist.\n    \"\"\"\n    content = path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    return parse_tasklist(content)"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1004,"cache_read_input_tokens":110560,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1004,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":6,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2a1ff615-267e-4275-a87d-ccb793108b67","uuid":"bc4215ee-62ce-4350-b69b-4de4592d1cfb"}

Expected canonical filenames like: \"\n            \"phase-1-tasklist.md, p1-tasklist.md, phase_1_tasklist.md, tasklist-p1.md\"\n        )\n\n    # Enrich phases with names\n    for phase in phases:\n        phase.name = _extract_phase_name(phase.file)\n\n    # Auto-detect end phase\n    if end_phase == 0:\n        end_phase = max(p.number for p in phases)\n\n    # Validate\n    validation = validate_phases(phases, start_phase, end_phase)\n    errors = validation[\"errors\"]\n    warnings = validation[\"warnings\"]\n    if errors:\n        for e in errors:\n            click.echo(e, err=True)\n        raise click.ClickException(f\"{len(errors)} phase file(s) missing.\")\n\n    for w in warnings:\n        click.echo(w, err=True)\n\n    config = SprintConfig(\n        index_path=index_path,\n        release_dir=index_path.parent,\n        phases=phases,\n        start_phase=start_phase,\n        end_phase=end_phase,\n        max_turns=max_turns,\n        model=model,\n        dry_run=dry_run,\n        permission_flag=permission_flag,\n        debug=debug,\n        stall_timeout=stall_timeout,\n        stall_action=stall_action,\n    )\n\n    # Validate that the requested range yields at least one active phase\n    if not config.active_phases:\n        available = [p.number for p in phases]\n        raise click.ClickException(\n            f\"No phases in range [{start_phase}, {end_phase}]. \"\n            f\"Available phase numbers: {available}\"\n        )\n\n    return config\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":170,"oldLines":3,"newStart":170,"newLines":110,"lines":["         )"," ","     return config","+","+","+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------","+# Tasklist parser — extracts task inventory from phase markdown","+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------","+","+_TASK_HEADING_RE = re.compile(","+    r\"^###\\s+(T\\d{2}\\.\\d{2})\\s*(?:--|-—|—)\\s*(.+)\",","+    re.MULTILINE,","+)","+","+_DEPENDENCY_RE = re.compile(","+    r\"\\*\\*Dependencies:\\*\\*\\s*(.*)\",","+    re.IGNORECASE,","+)","+","+_TASK_ID_REF_RE = re.compile(r\"T\\d{2}\\.\\d{2}\")","+","+_logger = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.sprint.config\")","+","+","+def parse_tasklist(content: str) -> list[TaskEntry]:","+    \"\"\"Parse phase tasklist markdown into a structured task inventory.","+","+    Extracts task ID, title, and dependency annotations from each","+    ``### T<PP>.<TT> -- Title`` block. Malformed input (missing headings,","+    invalid IDs, empty content) is handled gracefully — invalid blocks","+    are skipped with a logged warning.","+","+    Args:","+        content: Raw markdown text of a phase tasklist file.","+","+    Returns:","+        Ordered list of :class:`TaskEntry` instances.","+    \"\"\"","+    if not content or not content.strip():","+        return []","+","+    headings = list(_TASK_HEADING_RE.finditer(content))","+    if not headings:","+        _logger.warning(\"No task headings (### T<PP>.<TT>) found in tasklist content\")","+        return []","+","+    tasks: list[TaskEntry] = []","+    for i, match in enumerate(headings):","+        task_id = match.group(1)","+        title = match.group(2).strip()","+","+        # Extract the block body between this heading and the next (or EOF)","+        block_start = match.end()","+        block_end = headings[i + 1].start() if i + 1 < len(headings) else len(content)","+        block = content[block_start:block_end]","+","+        # Extract dependencies","+        dependencies: list[str] = []","+        dep_match = _DEPENDENCY_RE.search(block)","+        if dep_match:","+            dep_text = dep_match.group(1).strip()","+            if dep_text.lower() not in (\"none\", \"\"):","+                dependencies = _TASK_ID_REF_RE.findall(dep_text)","+","+        # Build description from the deliverables section if present","+        description = \"\"","+        for line in block.splitlines():","+            stripped = line.strip()","+            if stripped.startswith(\"**Deliverables:**\"):","+                # Grab the next non-empty line(s) after \"Deliverables:\"","+                idx = block.index(stripped) + len(stripped)","+                rest = block[idx:].strip()","+                desc_lines = []","+                for dline in rest.splitlines():","+                    dline = dline.strip()","+                    if not dline or dline.startswith(\"**\"):","+                        break","+                    # Strip leading \"- \" from markdown list items","+                    if dline.startswith(\"- \"):","+                        dline = dline[2:]","+                    desc_lines.append(dline)","+                description = \" \".join(desc_lines)","+                break","+","+        tasks.append(","+            TaskEntry(","+                task_id=task_id,","+                title=title,","+                description=description,","+                dependencies=dependencies,","+            )","+        )","+","+    return tasks","+","+","+def parse_tasklist_file(path: Path) -> list[TaskEntry]:","+    \"\"\"Read a phase tasklist file and parse it into a task inventory.","+","+    Args:","+        path: Path to the phase tasklist markdown file.","+","+    Returns:","+        Ordered list of :class:`TaskEntry` instances.","+","+    Raises:","+        FileNotFoundError: If the path does not exist.","+    \"\"\"","+    content = path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")","+    return parse_tasklist(content)"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

I need to add a per-TASK loop that runs WITHIN a phase. The existing per-phase loop stays as-is for backward compatibility; the new per-task loop is an alternative execution path.\n\nApproach:\n1. Add new models: TaskResult and TaskStatus for per-task outcomes\n2. Add a function `execute_tasks_for_phase()` that:\n   - Takes a list of TaskEntry + config + ledger\n   - For each task: checks budget via ledger, spawns subprocess, collects result\n   - Returns list of TaskResult\n3. This function is callable from the existing execute_sprint loop (or separately)\n\nKey design decisions:\n- TaskResult needs: task_entry, status (pass/fail/incomplete), turns_consumed, started_at, finished_at\n- TaskStatus enum: PASS, FAIL, INCOMPLETE (budget exhausted), SKIPPED (dependency failed)\n- Budget integration: ledger.debit(allocation) on launch, ledger.credit(unused) on completion\n- Starvation prevention: if budget insufficient for next task, HALT with remaining task IDs\n\nThe function signature:\n```python\ndef execute_tasks_for_phase(\n    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase: Phase,\n    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n) -> list[TaskResult]:\n```\n\nFor now this will be a testable orchestration function. timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .debug_logger import debug_log, setup_debug_logger\nfrom .diagnostics import DiagnosticCollector, FailureClassifier, ReportGenerator\nfrom .logging_ import SprintLogger\nfrom .models import (\n    MonitorState,\n    PhaseResult,\n    PhaseStatus,\n    SprintConfig,\n    SprintOutcome,\n    SprintResult,\n    TurnLedger,\n)\nfrom .monitor import OutputMonitor, detect_error_max_turns\nfrom .notify import notify_phase_complete, notify_sprint_complete\nfrom .process import ClaudeProcess, SignalHandler\nfrom .tmux import update_tail_pane\nfrom .tui import SprintTUI\n\n# Debug logger name for executor-specific events\n_DBG_NAME = \"superclaude.sprint.debug.executor\"\n\n\ndef check_budget_guard(ledger: TurnLedger | None) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"Pre-launch budget guard: returns a halt message if budget is insufficient.\n\n    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n    if the budget is too low to launch a subprocess.\n    \"\"\"\n    if ledger is None:\n        return None\n    if ledger.can_launch():\n        return None\n    return (\n        f\"Budget exhausted: {ledger.available()} turns remaining, \"\n        f\"minimum {ledger.minimum_allocation} required for launch\"\n    )\n\n\ndef execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n\n    For each active phase:\n    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n    2. Start output monitor thread\n    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n    5. Record PhaseResult\n    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n        raise SystemExit(\n            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n        )\n\n    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n    signal_handler.install()\n\n    setup_debug_logger(config)\n    import logging as _logging\n    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n\n    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n\n    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n\n    tui.start()\n\n    try:\n        for phase in config.active_phases:\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Reset monitor for this phase\n            output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n            monitor.reset(output_path)\n            monitor.start()\n\n            # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n            if config.tmux_session_name:\n                update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n\n            # Launch claude\n            proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase)\n            proc_manager.start()\n            started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n            deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n            logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n\n            debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n\n            # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n            # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n            _timed_out = False\n            _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n            _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n            while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n                    # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n                    _timed_out = True\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n\n                ms = monitor.state\n                _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n\n                debug_log(\n                    _dbg,\n                    \"poll_tick\",\n                    phase=phase.number,\n                    pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    poll_result=\"running\",\n                    elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n                    output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n                    growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n                    stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                    stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n                )\n\n                # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n                if (\n                    config.stall_timeout > 0\n                    and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n                    and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n                    and not _stall_acted\n                ):\n                    _stall_acted = True\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"watchdog_triggered\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        action=config.stall_action,\n                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    )\n                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n                        _timed_out = True\n                        proc_manager.terminate()\n                        break\n                    else:\n                        # warn action: log and continue\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n\n                # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n                if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n                    _stall_acted = False\n\n                # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n                # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n                try:\n                    tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n                except Exception as _tui_exc:\n                    import sys\n                    print(f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n\n            # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n            # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n            raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n            if _timed_out:\n                exit_code = 124\n            else:\n                exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n            monitor.stop()\n            finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"PHASE_END\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n            # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n            # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n            # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                logger.write_phase_interrupt(phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code)\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Determine phase status\n            status = _determine_phase_status(\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                result_file=config.result_file(phase),\n                output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n            )\n\n            # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n            error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n            error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n\n            phase_result = PhaseResult(\n                phase=phase,\n                status=status,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n                error_bytes=error_bytes,\n                last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n                files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n            )\n            sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"phase_complete\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                status=status.value,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # Log and notify\n            logger.write_phase_result(phase_result)\n            notify_phase_complete(phase_result)\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, None)\n\n            # Decide: continue or halt?\n            if status.is_failure:\n                # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n                try:\n                    collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n                    bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n                    classifier = FailureClassifier()\n                    bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n                    reporter = ReportGenerator()\n                    diag_path = config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n                    reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"diagnostic_report\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        category=bundle.category.value,\n                        path=str(diag_path),\n                    )\n                except Exception as _diag_exc:\n                    debug_log(_dbg, \"diagnostic_error\", phase=phase.number, error=str(_diag_exc))\n\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n                sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n                break\n\n        # Sprint finished\n        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n            # Verify all phases actually passed\n            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n\n        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n\n    finally:\n        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n        try:\n            monitor.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        if proc_manager is not None:\n            try:\n                proc_manager.terminate()\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n        try:\n            tui.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        try:\n            signal_handler.uninstall()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n    try:\n        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n    except OSError:\n        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n\n    if _exitcode != 0:\n        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n\n\ndef _determine_phase_status(\n    exit_code: int,\n    result_file: Path,\n    output_file: Path,\n) -> PhaseStatus:\n    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n\n    Priority:\n    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n    \"\"\"\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    if result_file.exists():\n        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n        upper = content.upper()\n        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n        if has_halt:\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        if has_continue:\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":18,"oldLines":6,"newStart":18,"newLines":9,"lines":["     SprintConfig,","     SprintOutcome,","     SprintResult,","+    TaskEntry,","+    TaskResult,","+    TaskStatus,","     TurnLedger,"," )"," from .monitor import OutputMonitor, detect_error_max_turns"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Returns task results and a list\n    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n\n    Args:\n        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n        config: Sprint configuration.\n        phase: The Phase being executed.\n        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n\n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n    \"\"\"\n    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n    remaining: list[str] = []\n\n    if not tasks:\n        return results, remaining\n\n    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # For testing,\n    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Build a task-specific prompt\n    prompt = (\n        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n    )\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n    proc.config = config\n    proc.phase = phase\n    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n    _Base.__init__(\n        proc,\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n    )\n    proc.start()\n    proc.wait()\n    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":961,"cache_read_input_tokens":119432,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":961,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2a1ff615-267e-4275-a87d-ccb793108b67","uuid":"277c1d4c-c4ab-45bd-b977-54b1a82d50c5"}
