dataclass\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .debug_logger import debug_log, setup_debug_logger\nfrom .diagnostics import DiagnosticCollector, FailureClassifier, ReportGenerator\nfrom .logging_ import SprintLogger\nfrom .models import (\n    MonitorState,\n    PhaseResult,\n    PhaseStatus,\n    SprintConfig,\n    SprintOutcome,\n    SprintResult,\n    TaskEntry,\n    TaskResult,\n    TaskStatus,\n    TurnLedger,\n)\nfrom .monitor import OutputMonitor, detect_error_max_turns\nfrom .notify import notify_phase_complete, notify_sprint_complete\nfrom .process import ClaudeProcess, SignalHandler\nfrom .tmux import update_tail_pane\nfrom .tui import SprintTUI\n\n# Debug logger name for executor-specific events\n_DBG_NAME = \"superclaude.sprint.debug.executor\"\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# 4-Layer Subprocess Isolation\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n@dataclass\nclass IsolationLayers:\n    \"\"\"Configuration for the 4-layer subprocess isolation.\n\n    Each layer prevents cross-task state leakage:\n    1. scoped_work_dir: Restrict working directory to release dir\n    2. git_boundary: Set GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES to prevent upward traversal\n    3. empty_plugin_dir: Point CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR to an empty tempdir\n    4. restricted_settings: Set CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR to an isolated tempdir\n\n    The ``env_vars`` property returns a dict of environment variable overrides\n    that should be merged into the subprocess environment.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    scoped_work_dir: Path\n    git_boundary: Path\n    plugin_dir: Path\n    settings_dir: Path\n\n    @property\n    def env_vars(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"Return environment variable overrides for all 4 isolation layers.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\": str(self.scoped_work_dir),\n            \"GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES\": str(self.git_boundary),\n            \"CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR\": str(self.plugin_dir),\n            \"CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR\": str(self.settings_dir),\n        }\n\n    @property\n    def layers_active(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return list of active isolation layer names for verification.\"\"\"\n        active = []\n        if self.scoped_work_dir.exists():\n            active.append(\"scoped_work_dir\")\n        if self.git_boundary.exists():\n            active.append(\"git_boundary\")\n        if self.plugin_dir.exists():\n            active.append(\"empty_plugin_dir\")\n        if self.settings_dir.exists():\n            active.append(\"restricted_settings\")\n        return active\n\n\ndef setup_isolation(config: SprintConfig) -> IsolationLayers:\n    \"\"\"Create 4-layer isolation for subprocess execution.\n\n    Sets up:\n    1. Scoped working directory (the release dir)\n    2. Git boundary (prevents git operations above release dir)\n    3. Empty plugin directory (no plugins loaded)\n    4. Restricted settings directory (minimal settings)\n\n    All directories are created if they don't exist. The caller is\n    responsible for passing ``layers.env_vars`` to the subprocess.\n\n    Args:\n        config: Sprint configuration providing the release directory.\n\n    Returns:\n        IsolationLayers with all 4 layers configured.\n    \"\"\"\n    base = config.results_dir / \".isolation\"\n    base.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    plugin_dir = base / \"plugins\"\n    plugin_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\n    settings_dir = base / \"settings\"\n    settings_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\n    return IsolationLayers(\n        scoped_work_dir=config.release_dir,\n        git_boundary=config.release_dir,\n        plugin_dir=plugin_dir,\n        settings_dir=settings_dir,\n    )\n\n\ndef check_budget_guard(ledger: TurnLedger | None) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"Pre-launch budget guard: returns a halt message if budget is insufficient.\n\n    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n    if the budget is too low to launch a subprocess.\n    \"\"\"\n    if ledger is None:\n        return None\n    if ledger.can_launch():\n        return None\n    return (\n        f\"Budget exhausted: {ledger.available()} turns remaining, \"\n        f\"minimum {ledger.minimum_allocation} required for launch\"\n    )\n\n\ndef execute_phase_tasks(\n    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n    *,\n    _subprocess_factory=None,\n) -> tuple[list[TaskResult], list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\n\n    Iterates over a task inventory, spawning one subprocess per task with\n    budget allocation from the TurnLedger. Returns task results and a list\n    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n\n    Args:\n        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n        config: Sprint configuration.\n        phase: The Phase being executed.\n        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n\n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n    \"\"\"\n    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n    remaining: list[str] = []\n\n    if not tasks:\n        return results, remaining\n\n    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Budget check: can we launch?\n        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\n            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\n            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\n            for t in tasks[i:]:\n                results.append(\n                    TaskResult(\n                        task=t,\n                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\n                        started_at=started_at,\n                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n                    )\n                )\n            break\n\n        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\n        if ledger is not None:\n            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\n\n        # Spawn subprocess for this task\n        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n        else:\n            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n\n        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Determine task status from exit code\n        if exit_code == 0:\n            status = TaskStatus.PASS\n        elif exit_code == 124:\n            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        else:\n            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n\n        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\n        if ledger is not None:\n            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\n            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\n            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\n            if actual > pre_allocated:\n                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\n            elif actual < pre_allocated:\n                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\n\n        results.append(\n            TaskResult(\n                task=task,\n                status=status,\n                turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=output_bytes,\n            )\n        )\n\n    return results, remaining\n\n\ndef _run_task_subprocess(\n    task: TaskEntry,\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n    \"\"\"Run a single task in a subprocess. Returns (exit_code, turns, output_bytes).\n\n    This is the real implementation that spawns a ClaudeProcess. For testing,\n    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Build a task-specific prompt\n    prompt = (\n        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n    )\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n    proc.config = config\n    proc.phase = phase\n    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n    _Base.__init__(\n        proc,\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n    )\n    proc.start()\n    proc.wait()\n    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)\n\n\ndef execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n\n    For each active phase:\n    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n    2. Start output monitor thread\n    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n    5. Record PhaseResult\n    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n        raise SystemExit(\n            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n        )\n\n    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n    signal_handler.install()\n\n    setup_debug_logger(config)\n    import logging as _logging\n    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n\n    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n\n    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n\n    tui.start()\n\n    try:\n        for phase in config.active_phases:\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Reset monitor for this phase\n            output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n            monitor.reset(output_path)\n            monitor.start()\n\n            # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n            if config.tmux_session_name:\n                update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n\n            # Launch claude\n            proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase)\n            proc_manager.start()\n            started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n            deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n            logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n\n            debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n\n            # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n            # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n            _timed_out = False\n            _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n            _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n            while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n                    # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n                    _timed_out = True\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n\n                ms = monitor.state\n                _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n\n                debug_log(\n                    _dbg,\n                    \"poll_tick\",\n                    phase=phase.number,\n                    pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    poll_result=\"running\",\n                    elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n                    output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n                    growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n                    stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                    stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n                )\n\n                # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n                if (\n                    config.stall_timeout > 0\n                    and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n                    and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n                    and not _stall_acted\n                ):\n                    _stall_acted = True\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"watchdog_triggered\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        action=config.stall_action,\n                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    )\n                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n                        _timed_out = True\n                        proc_manager.terminate()\n                        break\n                    else:\n                        # warn action: log and continue\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n\n                # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n                if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n                    _stall_acted = False\n\n                # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n                # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n                try:\n                    tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n                except Exception as _tui_exc:\n                    import sys\n                    print(f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n\n            # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n            # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n            raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n            if _timed_out:\n                exit_code = 124\n            else:\n                exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n            monitor.stop()\n            finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"PHASE_END\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n            # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n            # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n            # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                logger.write_phase_interrupt(phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code)\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Determine phase status\n            status = _determine_phase_status(\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                result_file=config.result_file(phase),\n                output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n            )\n\n            # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n            error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n            error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n\n            phase_result = PhaseResult(\n                phase=phase,\n                status=status,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n                error_bytes=error_bytes,\n                last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n                files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n            )\n            sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"phase_complete\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                status=status.value,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # Log and notify\n            logger.write_phase_result(phase_result)\n            notify_phase_complete(phase_result)\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, None)\n\n            # Decide: continue or halt?\n            if status.is_failure:\n                # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n                try:\n                    collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n                    bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n                    classifier = FailureClassifier()\n                    bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n                    reporter = ReportGenerator()\n                    diag_path = config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n                    reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"diagnostic_report\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        category=bundle.category.value,\n                        path=str(diag_path),\n                    )\n                except Exception as _diag_exc:\n                    debug_log(_dbg, \"diagnostic_error\", phase=phase.number, error=str(_diag_exc))\n\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n                sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n                break\n\n        # Sprint finished\n        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n            # Verify all phases actually passed\n            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n\n        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n\n    finally:\n        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n        try:\n            monitor.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        if proc_manager is not None:\n            try:\n                proc_manager.terminate()\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n        try:\n            tui.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        try:\n            signal_handler.uninstall()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n    try:\n        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n    except OSError:\n        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n\n    if _exitcode != 0:\n        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n\n\ndef _determine_phase_status(\n    exit_code: int,\n    result_file: Path,\n    output_file: Path,\n) -> PhaseStatus:\n    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n\n    Priority:\n    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n    \"\"\"\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    if result_file.exists():\n        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n        upper = content.upper()\n        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n        if has_halt:\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        if has_continue:\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":117,"oldLines":6,"newStart":117,"newLines":127,"lines":[" \"\"\"Tests for per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\"\"\"\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _make_tasks(count: int = 3) -> list[TaskEntry]:\n        return [\n            TaskEntry(\n                task_id=f\"T02.{i:02d}\",\n                title=f\"Task {i}\",\n                dependencies=[f\"T02.{i-1:02d}\"] if i > 1 else [],\n            )\n            for i in range(1, count + 1)\n        ]\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _pass_factory(task, config, phase):\n        \"\"\"Subprocess factory that always succeeds with 3 turns consumed.\"\"\"\n        return (0, 3, 1024)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _fail_factory(task, config, phase):\n        \"\"\"Subprocess factory that always fails.\"\"\"\n        return (1, 5, 512)\n\n    def test_per_task_spawns_one_subprocess_per_task(self, tmp_path):\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(3)\n\n        spawn_count = [0]\n\n        def counting_factory(task, config, phase):\n            spawn_count[0] += 1\n            return (0, 2, 100)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, _subprocess_factory=counting_factory\n        )\n        assert spawn_count[0] == 3\n        assert len(results) == 3\n        assert remaining == []\n\n    def test_per_task_all_pass(self, tmp_path):\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(2)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, _subprocess_factory=self._pass_factory\n        )\n        assert all(r.status == TaskStatus.PASS for r in results)\n        assert remaining == []\n\n    def test_per_task_budget_prevents_starvation(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"If budget insufficient for next task, HALT with remaining task IDs.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(3)\n\n        # Budget allows only 1 task launch (min_allocation=5, budget=8)\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=8, minimum_allocation=5)\n\n        def consume_factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 5, 100)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=consume_factory,\n        )\n        # First task succeeds, next two should be skipped due to budget\n        passed = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS]\n        skipped = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED]\n        assert len(passed) == 1\n        assert len(skipped) == 2\n        assert remaining == [\"T02.02\", \"T02.03\"]\n\n    def test_per_task_budget_debit_credit(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"TurnLedger debit/credit occurs per-task.\"\"\"\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(1)\n\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=50, minimum_allocation=5)\n        initial = ledger.available()\n\n        results, _ = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=self._pass_factory,\n        )\n        # Factory returns 3 turns consumed; ledger should reflect that\n        assert ledger.available() == initial - 3\n\n    def test_per_task_empty_inventory(self, tmp_path):\n        config = orchestration.\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.sprint.executor import (\n    _determine_phase_status,\n    aggregate_task_results,\n    AggregatedPhaseReport,\n    check_budget_guard,\n    execute_phase_tasks,\n    execute_sprint,\n    setup_isolation,\n    IsolationLayers,\n)\nfrom superclaude.cli.sprint.models import (\n    Phase,\n    PhaseStatus,\n    SprintConfig,\n    SprintOutcome,\n    TaskEntry,\n    TaskResult,\n    TaskStatus,\n    TurnLedger,\n)\n\n\ndef _make_config(tmp_path: Path, num_phases: int = 2) -> SprintConfig:\n    phases = []\n    for i in range(1, num_phases + 1):\n        pf = tmp_path / f\"phase-{i}-tasklist.md\"\n        pf.write_text(f\"# Phase {i}\\n\")\n        phases.append(Phase(number=i, file=pf, name=f\"Phase {i}\"))\n\n    index = tmp_path / \"tasklist-index.md\"\n    index.write_text(\"index\\n\")\n\n    return SprintConfig(\n        index_path=index,\n        release_dir=tmp_path,\n        phases=phases,\n        start_phase=1,\n        end_phase=num_phases,\n        max_turns=5,\n    )\n\n\nclass TestDeterminePhaseStatus:\n    \"\"\"Test the 7-level status priority chain.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_timeout_exit_code(self, tmp_path):\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n        output_file.write_text(\"some output\")\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=124,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n\n    def test_timeout_overrides_result_file(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Timeout (exit 124) takes priority over result file content.\"\"\"\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\")\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=124,\n            result_file=result_file,\n output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n        output_file.write_text(\"some output\")\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=1,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    def test_nonzero_exit_overrides_continue_signal(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Non-zero exit takes priority over CONTINUE signal.\"\"\"\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\")\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=1,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    def test_halt_signal(self, tmp_path):\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\")\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.HALT\n\n    def test_continue_signal(self, tmp_path):\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        result_file.write_text(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\")\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.PASS\n\n    def test_yaml_status_pass(self, tmp_path):\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        result_file.write_text(\"---\\nstatus: PASS\\n---\\n\")\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.PASS\n\n    def test_yaml_status_fail(self, tmp_path):\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        result_file.write_text(\"---\\nstatus: FAIL\\n---\\n\")\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.HALT\n\n    def test_result_file_no_signals(self, tmp_path):\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        result_file.write_text(\"Some result content without signals\")\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    def test_no_result_file_with_output(self, tmp_path):\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        # Don't create result file\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n        output_file.write_text(\"Claude output here\")\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    def test_no_result_no_output(self, tmp_path):\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n        # Neither file exists\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    def test_empty_output_file(self, tmp_path):\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n        output_file.write_text(\"\")  # empty\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    # --- Regression tests for case-sensitivity and signal conflict fixes ---\n\n    def test_continue_signal_case_insensitive(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Lowercase EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: continue must still produce PASS.\"\"\"\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        result_file.write_text(\"exit_recommendation: continue\\n\")\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.PASS\n\n    def test_halt_signal_case_insensitive(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"Lowercase EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: halt must still produce HALT.\"\"\"\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        result_file.write_text(\"exit_recommendation: halt\\n\")\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.HALT\n\n    def test_both_continue_and_halt_halt_wins(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"When both EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens appear, HALT must win (safer outcome).\"\"\"\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        # CONTINUE appears before HALT — HALT must still win\n        result_file.write_text(\n            \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\"\n            \"Some tasks failed.\\n\"\n            \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\\n\"\n        )\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.HALT\n\n    def test_partial_status_produces_halt(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"PARTIAL status in result file must produce HALT (existing behavior, regression guard).\"\"\"\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        result_file.write_text(\"---\\nstatus: PARTIAL\\n---\\n\")\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n test_reclassification_pass_no_report_with_error_max_turns(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"PASS_NO_REPORT + error_max_turns → INCOMPLETE.\"\"\"\n        result_file = tmp_path / \"result.md\"\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n        output_file.write_text(\n            '{\"type\":\"content\",\"text\":\"working...\"}\\n'\n            '{\"type\":\"result\",\"subtype\":\"error_max_turns\"}\\n'\n        )\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n            result_file=result_file,\n            output_file=output_file,\n        )\n        assert status == PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n\n    def test_reclassification_pass_no_report_without_error_max_turns(self, tmp_path):\n \"\"\"Tests for per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\"\"\"\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _make_tasks(count: int = 3) -> list[TaskEntry]:\n        return [\n            TaskEntry(\n                task_id=f\"T02.{i:02d}\",\n                title=f\"Task {i}\",\n                dependencies=[f\"T02.{i-1:02d}\"] if i > 1 else [],\n            )\n            for i in range(1, count + 1)\n        ]\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _pass_factory(task, config, phase):\n        \"\"\"Subprocess factory that always succeeds with 3 turns consumed.\"\"\"\n        return (0, 3, 1024)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _fail_factory(task, config, phase):\n        \"\"\"Subprocess factory that always fails.\"\"\"\n        return (1, 5, 512)\n\n    def test_per_task_spawns_one_subprocess_per_task(self, tmp_path):\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(3)\n\n        spawn_count = [0]\n\n        def counting_factory(task, config, phase):\n            spawn_count[0] += 1\n            return (0, 2, 100)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(3)\n\n        # Budget allows only 1 task launch (min_allocation=5, budget=8)\n        ledger = TurnLedger(initial_budget=8, minimum_allocation=5)\n\n        def consume_factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 5, 100)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=consume_factory,\n        )\n        # First task succeeds, next two should be skipped due to budget\n        passed = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS]\n        skipped = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED]\n        assert len(passed) == 1\n        assert len(skipped) == 2\n        assert 4-Layer Subprocess Isolation\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n@dataclass\nclass IsolationLayers:\n    \"\"\"Configuration for the 4-layer subprocess isolation.\n\n    Each layer prevents cross-task state leakage:\n    1. scoped_work_dir: Restrict working directory to release dir\n    2. git_boundary: Set GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES to prevent upward traversal\n    3. empty_plugin_dir: Point CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR to an empty tempdir\n    4. restricted_settings: Set CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR to an isolated tempdir\n\n    The ``env_vars`` property returns a dict of environment variable overrides\n    that should be merged into the subprocess environment.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    scoped_work_dir: Path\n    git_boundary: Path\n    plugin_dir: Path\n    settings_dir: Path\n\n    @property\n    def env_vars(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"Return environment variable overrides for all 4 isolation layers.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"CLAUDE_WORK_DIR\": str(self.scoped_work_dir),\n            \"GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES\": str(self.git_boundary),\n            \"CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DIR\": str(self.plugin_dir),\n            \"CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR\": str(self.settings_dir),\n        }\n\n    @property\n    def layers_active(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return list of active isolation layer names for verification.\"\"\"\n        active = []\n        if self.scoped_work_dir.exists():\n            active.append(\"scoped_work_dir\")\n        if self.git_boundary.exists():\n            active.append(\"git_boundary\")\n        if self.plugin_dir.exists():\n            active.append(\"empty_plugin_dir\")\n        if self.settings_dir.exists():\n            active.append(\"restricted_settings\")\n        return active\n\n\ndef setup_isolation(config: SprintConfig) -> IsolationLayers:\n    \"\"\"Create 4-layer isolation for subprocess execution.\n\n    Sets up:\n    1. Scoped working directory (the release dir)\n    2. Git boundary (prevents git operations above release dir)\n    3. Empty plugin directory (no plugins loaded)\n    4. Restricted settings directory (minimal settings)\n\n    All directories are created if they don't exist. The caller is\n    responsible for passing ``layers.env_vars`` to the subprocess.\n\n    Args:\n        config: Sprint configuration providing the release directory.\n\n    Returns:\n        IsolationLayers with all 4 layers configured.\n    \"\"\"\n    base = config.results_dir / \".isolation\"\n    base.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    plugin_dir = base / \"plugins\"\n    plugin_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\n    settings_dir = base / \"settings\"\n    settings_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\n    return IsolationLayers(\n        scoped_work_dir=config.release_dir,\n        git_boundary=config.release_dir,\n        plugin_dir=plugin_dir,\n        settings_dir=settings_dir,\n    )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Result Aggregation — runner-constructed phase reports\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n@dataclass\nclass AggregatedPhaseReport:\n    \"\"\"Runner-constructed phase report from collected TaskResults.\n\n    This report is built by the runner, not by parsing agent self-reported\n    output, ensuring accurate task outcome tracking even when subprocesses\n    are budget-exhausted.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    phase_number: int\n    tasks_total: int = 0\n    tasks_passed: int = 0\n    tasks_failed: int = 0\n    tasks_incomplete: int = 0\n    tasks_skipped: int = 0\n    total_turns_consumed: int = 0\n    total_duration_seconds: float = 0.0\n    task_results: list[TaskResult] = field(default_factory=list)\n    remaining_task_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n    @property\n    def status(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Overall phase status: PASS, FAIL, or PARTIAL.\"\"\"\n        if self.tasks_total == 0:\n            return \"PASS\"\n        if self.tasks_passed == self.tasks_total:\n            return \"PASS\"\n        if self.tasks_passed == 0:\n            return \"FAIL\"\n        return \"PARTIAL\"\n\n    def to_markdown(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Render the report as a markdown string with YAML frontmatter.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            \"---\",\n            f\"phase: {self.phase_number}\",\n            f\"status: {self.status}\",\n            f\"tasks_total: {self.tasks_total}\",\n            f\"tasks_passed: {self.tasks_passed}\",\n            f\"tasks_failed: {self.tasks_failed}\",\n            \"---\",\n            \"\",\n            f\"# Phase {self.phase_number} — Aggregated Task Report\",\n            \"\",\n            \"| Task ID | Title | Status | Turns | Duration |\",\n            \"|---------|-------|--------|-------|----------|\",\n        ]\n        for tr in self.task_results:\n            dur = f\"{tr.duration_seconds:.1f}s\"\n            lines.append(\n                f\"| {tr.task.task_id} | {tr.task.title} | {tr.status.value} \"\n                f\"| {tr.turns_consumed} | {dur} |\"\n            )\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        lines.append(f\"**Total turns consumed:** {self.total_turns_consumed}\")\n        lines.append(f\"**Total duration:** {self.total_duration_seconds:.1f}s\")\n\n        if self.remaining_task_ids:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Remaining Tasks (Budget Exhausted)\")\n            for tid in self.remaining_task_ids:\n                lines.append(f\"- {tid}\")\n\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        if self.status == \"PASS\":\n            lines.append(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\")\n        else:\n            lines.append(\"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines) + \"\\n\"\n\n\ndef aggregate_task_results(\n    phase_number: int,\n    task_results: list[TaskResult],\n    remaining_task_ids: list[str] | None = None,\n) -> AggregatedPhaseReport:\n    \"\"\"Aggregate individual TaskResults into a runner-constructed PhaseReport.\n\n    This function is the runner's authoritative source of task outcomes.\n    It does not rely on agent self-reporting.\n\n    Args:\n        phase_number: The phase number being aggregated.\n        task_results: List of TaskResult from execute_phase_tasks().\n        remaining_task_ids: Task IDs that were not attempted due to budget.\n\n    Returns:\n        AggregatedPhaseReport with computed counts and status.\n    \"\"\"\n    report = AggregatedPhaseReport(\n        phase_number=phase_number,\n        task_results=task_results,\n        remaining_task_ids=remaining_task_ids or [],\n    )\n\n    report.tasks_total = len(task_results) + len(report.remaining_task_ids)\n    report.tasks_passed = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS\n    )\n    report.tasks_failed = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.FAIL\n    )\n    report.tasks_incomplete = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n    )\n    report.tasks_skipped = sum(\n        1 for r in task_results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED\n    )\n    report.total_turns_consumed = sum(r.turns_consumed for r in task_results)\n    report.total_duration_seconds = sum(\n        r.duration_seconds for r in task_results\n    )\n\n    return report\n\n\ndef check_budget_guard(ledger: TurnLedger | None) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"Pre-launch budget guard: returns a halt message if budget is insufficient.\n\n    Returns None if launch is allowed, or a descriptive message string\n    if the budget is too low to launch a subprocess.\n    \"\"\"\n    if ledger is None:\n        return None\n    if ledger.can_launch():\n        return None\n    return (\n        f\"Budget exhausted: {ledger.available()} turns remaining, \"\n        f\"minimum {ledger.minimum_allocation} required for launch\"\n    )\n\n\ndef execute_phase_tasks(\n    tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n    *,\n    _subprocess_factory=None,\n) -> tuple[list[TaskResult], list[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\n\n    Iterates over a task inventory, spawning one subprocess per task with\n    budget allocation from the TurnLedger. Returns task results and a list\n    of remaining (unattempted) task IDs if budget was exhausted.\n\n    Args:\n        tasks: Ordered list of TaskEntry from the tasklist parser.\n        config: Sprint configuration.\n        phase: The Phase being executed.\n        ledger: Optional TurnLedger for budget tracking.\n        _subprocess_factory: Optional callable for testing; signature\n            ``(task, config, phase) -> (exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes)``.\n\n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (results, remaining_task_ids). remaining_task_ids is non-empty\n        only when the loop halted due to budget exhaustion.\n    \"\"\"\n    results: list[TaskResult] = []\n    remaining: list[str] = []\n\n    if not tasks:\n        return results, remaining\n\n    for i, task in enumerate(tasks):\n        started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Budget check: can we launch?\n        if ledger is not None and not ledger.can_launch():\n            # Mark this and all subsequent tasks as skipped\n            remaining = [t.task_id for t in tasks[i:]]\n            for t in tasks[i:]:\n                results.append(\n                    TaskResult(\n                        task=t,\n                        status=TaskStatus.SKIPPED,\n                        started_at=started_at,\n                        finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n                    )\n                )\n            break\n\n        # Debit the minimum allocation upfront\n        if ledger is not None:\n            ledger.debit(ledger.minimum_allocation)\n\n        # Spawn subprocess for this task\n        if _subprocess_factory is not None:\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _subprocess_factory(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n        else:\n            # Default: delegate to ClaudeProcess (real execution)\n            exit_code, turns_consumed, output_bytes = _run_task_subprocess(\n                task, config, phase\n            )\n\n        finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Determine task status from exit code\n        if exit_code == 0:\n            status = TaskStatus.PASS\n        elif exit_code == 124:\n            status = TaskStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        else:\n            status = TaskStatus.FAIL\n\n        # Reconcile budget: debit actual consumption, credit back pre-allocation\n        if ledger is not None:\n            # We pre-debited minimum_allocation; now adjust for actual turns\n            actual = max(turns_consumed, 0)\n            pre_allocated = ledger.minimum_allocation\n            if actual > pre_allocated:\n                ledger.debit(actual - pre_allocated)\n            elif actual < pre_allocated:\n                ledger.credit(pre_allocated - actual)\n\n        results.append(\n            TaskResult(\n                task=task,\n                status=status,\n                turns_consumed=turns_consumed,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=output_bytes,\n            )\n        )\n\n    return results, remaining\n\n\ndef _run_task_subprocess(\n    task: TaskEntry,\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    phase,\n) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n    \"\"\"Run a single task in a subprocess. Returns (exit_code, turns, output_bytes).\n\n    This is the real implementation that spawns a ClaudeProcess. For testing,\n    callers of execute_phase_tasks pass _subprocess_factory instead.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Build a task-specific prompt\n    prompt = (\n        f\"Execute task {task.task_id}: {task.title}\\n\"\n        f\"From phase file: {phase.file}\\n\"\n        f\"Description: {task.description}\\n\"\n    )\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess.__new__(ClaudeProcess)\n    proc.config = config\n    proc.phase = phase\n    from superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _Base\n    _Base.__init__(\n        proc,\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n        error_file=config.error_file(phase),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=config.max_turns * 120 + 300,\n        output_format=\"stream-json\",\n    )\n    proc.start()\n    proc.wait()\n    exit_code = proc._process.returncode if proc._process else -1\n    output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n    output_bytes = output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() else 0\n    # Turn counting is wired separately in T02.06\n    return (exit_code if exit_code is not None else -1, 0, output_bytes)\n\n\ndef execute_sprint(config: SprintConfig):\n    \"\"\"Main orchestration loop.\n\n    For each active phase:\n    1. Launch claude -p subprocess\n    2. Start output monitor thread\n    3. Update TUI in a polling loop until process exits (with timeout)\n    4. Parse result file for CONTINUE/HALT\n    5. Record PhaseResult\n    6. Decide whether to continue or halt\n    \"\"\"\n    # Pre-flight: verify claude binary is available before starting TUI/logging\n    if shutil.which(\"claude\") is None:\n        raise SystemExit(\n            \"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH. \"\n            \"Install Claude Code CLI before running sprint.\"\n        )\n\n    signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n    signal_handler.install()\n\n    setup_debug_logger(config)\n    import logging as _logging\n    _dbg = _logging.getLogger(_DBG_NAME)\n\n    logger = SprintLogger(config)\n    tui = SprintTUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor(Path(\"/dev/null\"))  # reset per phase\n    proc_manager: ClaudeProcess | None = None\n\n    sprint_result = SprintResult(config=config)\n    logger.write_header(sprint_result)\n\n    tui.start()\n\n    try:\n        for phase in config.active_phases:\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Reset monitor for this phase\n            output_path = config.output_file(phase)\n            monitor.reset(output_path)\n            monitor.start()\n\n            # Update tmux tail pane if running in tmux\n            if config.tmux_session_name:\n                update_tail_pane(config.tmux_session_name, output_path)\n\n            # Launch claude\n            proc_manager = ClaudeProcess(config, phase)\n            proc_manager.start()\n            started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            # Use monotonic clock for deadline enforcement to be immune to NTP adjustments\n            deadline = time.monotonic() + proc_manager.timeout_seconds\n            logger.write_phase_start(phase, started_at)\n\n            debug_log(_dbg, \"PHASE_BEGIN\", phase=phase.number, file=str(phase.file))\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n\n            # Poll loop: wait for process to finish while updating TUI\n            # Enforces monotonic timeout via deadline check.\n            _timed_out = False\n            _stall_acted = False  # single-fire guard for watchdog\n            _poll_start = time.monotonic()\n            while proc_manager._process.poll() is None:\n                if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n                    # Timeout reached: kill the process, exit loop\n                    _timed_out = True\n                    proc_manager.terminate()\n                    break\n\n                ms = monitor.state\n                _elapsed = time.monotonic() - _poll_start\n\n                debug_log(\n                    _dbg,\n                    \"poll_tick\",\n                    phase=phase.number,\n                    pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    poll_result=\"running\",\n                    elapsed=round(_elapsed, 1),\n                    output_bytes=ms.output_bytes,\n                    growth_rate=round(ms.growth_rate_bps, 1),\n                    stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                    stall_status=ms.stall_status,\n                )\n\n                # --- Watchdog: stall timeout check ---\n                if (\n                    config.stall_timeout > 0\n                    and ms.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n                    and ms.events_received > 0  # don't trigger during startup\n                    and not _stall_acted\n                ):\n                    _stall_acted = True\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"watchdog_triggered\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        action=config.stall_action,\n                        stall_seconds=round(ms.stall_seconds, 1),\n                        pid=proc_manager._process.pid,\n                    )\n                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — killing phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n                        _timed_out = True\n                        proc_manager.terminate()\n                        break\n                    else:\n                        # warn action: log and continue\n                        import sys\n                        print(\n                            f\"[WATCHDOG] Stall detected ({ms.stall_seconds:.0f}s > \"\n                            f\"{config.stall_timeout}s) — warning for phase {phase.number}\",\n                            file=sys.stderr,\n                        )\n\n                # Reset single-fire guard when output resumes\n                if _stall_acted and ms.stall_seconds == 0.0:\n                    _stall_acted = False\n\n                # Update TUI at ~2 Hz (monitor thread handles data extraction)\n                # Wrap in try/except so a display glitch cannot abort the sprint\n                try:\n                    tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, phase)\n                except Exception as _tui_exc:\n                    import sys\n                    print(f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {_tui_exc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n\n            # Safely read exit code: returncode may be None if terminate raced.\n            # Use _timed_out flag instead of assigning directly to returncode.\n            raw_rc = proc_manager._process.returncode\n            if _timed_out:\n                exit_code = 124\n            else:\n                exit_code = raw_rc if raw_rc is not None else -1\n            monitor.stop()\n            finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            _phase_dur = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"PHASE_END\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # If shutdown was requested during the poll loop, classify as\n            # INTERRUPTED rather than letting _determine_phase_status see\n            # exit_code=-1 (None→-1 fallback) and return PhaseStatus.ERROR,\n            # which would incorrectly set the outcome to HALTED.\n            if signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                logger.write_phase_interrupt(phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code)\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Determine phase status\n            status = _determine_phase_status(\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                result_file=config.result_file(phase),\n                output_file=config.output_file(phase),\n            )\n\n            # Collect stderr size for telemetry\n            error_file = config.error_file(phase)\n            error_bytes = error_file.stat().st_size if error_file.exists() else 0\n\n            phase_result = PhaseResult(\n                phase=phase,\n                status=status,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=finished_at,\n                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n                error_bytes=error_bytes,\n                last_task_id=monitor.state.last_task_id,\n                files_changed=monitor.state.files_changed,\n            )\n            sprint_result.phase_results.append(phase_result)\n\n            debug_log(\n                _dbg,\n                \"phase_complete\",\n                phase=phase.number,\n                status=status.value,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                duration=round(_phase_dur, 1),\n            )\n\n            # Log and notify\n            logger.write_phase_result(phase_result)\n            notify_phase_complete(phase_result)\n\n            tui.update(sprint_result, monitor.state, None)\n\n            # Decide: continue or halt?\n            if status.is_failure:\n                # Collect diagnostics for the failed phase\n                try:\n                    collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n                    bundle = collector.collect(phase, phase_result, monitor.state)\n                    classifier = FailureClassifier()\n                    bundle.category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n                    reporter = ReportGenerator()\n                    diag_path = config.results_dir / f\"phase-{phase.number}-diagnostic.md\"\n                    reporter.write(bundle, diag_path)\n                    debug_log(\n                        _dbg,\n                        \"diagnostic_report\",\n                        phase=phase.number,\n                        category=bundle.category.value,\n                        path=str(diag_path),\n                    )\n                except Exception as _diag_exc:\n                    debug_log(_dbg, \"diagnostic_error\", phase=phase.number, error=str(_diag_exc))\n\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.HALTED\n                sprint_result.halt_phase = phase.number\n                break\n\n        # Sprint finished\n        sprint_result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS:\n            # Verify all phases actually passed\n            if not all(r.status.is_success for r in sprint_result.phase_results):\n                sprint_result.outcome = SprintOutcome.ERROR\n\n        tui.update(sprint_result, MonitorState(), None)\n        logger.write_summary(sprint_result)\n        notify_sprint_complete(sprint_result)\n\n    finally:\n        # Ensure monitor thread and subprocess are cleaned up even on exception.\n        # Each step is independent so one failure does not prevent others.\n        try:\n            monitor.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        if proc_manager is not None:\n            try:\n                proc_manager.terminate()\n            except Exception:\n                pass\n        try:\n            tui.stop()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        try:\n            signal_handler.uninstall()\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n    # Write sentinel exit code file so tmux caller can read the outcome\n    _exitcode = 0 if sprint_result.outcome == SprintOutcome.SUCCESS else 1\n    try:\n        (config.release_dir / \".sprint-exitcode\").write_text(str(_exitcode))\n    except OSError:\n        pass  # best-effort; do not mask the real exit\n\n    if _exitcode != 0:\n        raise SystemExit(_exitcode)\n\n\ndef _determine_phase_status(\n    exit_code: int,\n    result_file: Path,\n    output_file: Path,\n) -> PhaseStatus:\n    \"\"\"Parse result file and exit code to determine phase status.\n\n    Priority:\n    1. Timeout (exit 124) -> TIMEOUT\n    2. Non-zero exit -> ERROR\n    3. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT -> HALT\n    4. Result file with EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE -> PASS\n    5. Result file with status: PASS/FAIL -> PASS/HALT\n    6. No result file but output exists -> PASS_NO_REPORT\n    7. No result file and no output -> ERROR\n    \"\"\"\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n\n    if result_file.exists():\n        content = result_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n        # Use case-insensitive search for EXIT_RECOMMENDATION tokens to handle\n        # model output that varies in casing. When both CONTINUE and HALT appear\n        # (conflicting signals), HALT wins — the stronger/safer outcome.\n        upper = content.upper()\n        has_continue = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in upper\n        has_halt = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in upper\n        if has_halt:\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        if has_continue:\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PASS\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.PASS\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*FAIL(?:ED|URE)?\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        # PARTIAL result means tasks did not fully complete — treat as halt\n        if re.search(r\"status:\\s*PARTIAL\\b\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return PhaseStatus.HALT\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    if output_file.exists() and output_file.stat().st_size > 0:\n        # Check for budget exhaustion: a subprocess that exits 0 but hit\n        # error_max_turns produced no useful result — reclassify as INCOMPLETE\n        # to trigger HALT instead of silent continuation.\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_file):\n            return PhaseStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        return PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    return PhaseStatus.ERROR\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":5,"oldLines":7,"newStart":5,"newLines":7,"lines":[" import re"," import shutil"," import time","-from dataclasses import dataclass","+from dataclasses import dataclass, field"," from datetime import datetime, timezone"," from pathlib import Path"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

The monitor extracts task IDs,\ntool names, and file-change signals from these events to drive the TUI.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport re\nimport threading\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .debug_logger import debug_log\nfrom .models import MonitorState\n\n_dbg = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.sprint.debug.monitor\")\n\n# Patterns to extract from stringified event content\nTASK_ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"T\\d{2}\\.\\d{2}\")\nTOOL_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    r\"\\b(Read|Edit|MultiEdit|Write|Grep|Glob|Bash|TodoWrite|TodoRead|Task)\\b\"\n)\nFILES_CHANGED_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    r\"(?:modified|created|edited|wrote|updated)\\s+[`'\\\"]?([^\\s`'\\\"]+\\.\\w+)\"\n)\n\n# Pattern for detecting budget exhaustion in NDJSON output\nERROR_MAX_TURNS_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\"subtype\"\\s*:\\s*\"error_max_turns\"')\n\n\ndef detect_error_max_turns(output_path: Path) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if the last NDJSON line indicates budget exhaustion.\n\n    Scans the last non-empty line of the output file for the\n    ``\"subtype\":\"error_max_turns\"`` pattern, which signals that a\n    subprocess exhausted its turn budget.\n\n    Returns True if error_max_turns is detected, False otherwise.\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        content = output_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    except (FileNotFoundError, OSError):\n        return False\n\n    if not content.strip():\n        return False\n\n    # Get last non-empty line\n    lines = content.strip().splitlines()\n    for line in reversed(lines):\n        line = line.strip()\n        if line:\n            return bool(ERROR_MAX_TURNS_PATTERN.search(line))\n\n    return False\n\n\nclass OutputMonitor:\n    \"\"\"Background thread that watches a stream-json output file.\n\n    Reads incremental bytes, splits on newlines, and parses each complete\n    line as JSON.  Partial lines are buffered across poll cycles so no\n    data is lost when a write straddles a poll boundary.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, output_path: Path, poll_interval: float = 0.5):\n        self.output_path = output_path\n        self.poll_interval = poll_interval\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self._thread: threading.Thread | None = None\n        self._last_read_pos: int = 0\n        self._line_buffer: str = \"\"\n        self._seen_files: set[str] = set()\n\n    def start(self):\n        \"\"\"Start the monitor thread.\"\"\"\n        self._stop_event.clear()\n        self._last_read_pos = 0\n        self._line_buffer = \"\"\n        self._seen_files.clear()\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n        self._thread = threading.Thread(\n            target=self._poll_loop,\n            daemon=True,\n            name=\"output-monitor\",\n        )\n        self._thread.start()\n\n    def stop(self):\n        \"\"\"Stop the monitor thread.\"\"\"\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        if self._thread:\n            self._thread.join(timeout=2.0)\n\n    def reset(self, new_output_path: Path):\n        \"\"\"Reset for a new phase (new output file).\"\"\"\n        self.output_path = new_output_path\n        self._last_read_pos = 0\n        self._line_buffer = \"\"\n        self._seen_files.clear()\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n\n    def _poll_loop(self):\n        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n            self._poll_once()\n            self._stop_event.wait(self.poll_interval)\n\n    def _poll_once(self):\n        now = time.monotonic()\n\n        try:\n            size = self.output_path.stat().st_size\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            return\n\n        self.state.output_bytes_prev = self.state.output_bytes\n        self.state.output_bytes = size\n\n        if size > self._last_read_pos:\n            # New data available — read incremental chunk\n            chunk = self._read_new_chunk(size)\n            if chunk:\n                self._process_chunk(chunk, now)\n        else:\n            # No growth — update stall counter\n            self.state.stall_seconds = now - self.state.last_event_time\n\n        debug_log(\n            _dbg,\n            \"output_file_stat\",\n            path=str(self.output_path),\n            size=size,\n            events_received=self.state.events_received,\n            last_event_time=round(self.state.last_event_time, 1),\n        )\n\n        # Growth rate: exponential moving average\n        delta = self.state.output_bytes - self.state.output_bytes_prev\n        alpha = 0.3\n        self.state.growth_rate_bps = (\n            alpha * (delta / self.poll_interval)\n            + (1 - alpha) * self.state.growth_rate_bps\n        )\n\n    def _read_new_chunk(self, current_size: int) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Read only the bytes added since last poll.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            with open(self.output_path, errors=\"replace\") as f:\n                f.seek(self._last_read_pos)\n                chunk = f.read(current_size - self._last_read_pos)\n                self._last_read_pos = current_size\n                return chunk\n        except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError):\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _process_chunk(self, chunk: str, now: float):\n        \"\"\"Split chunk into lines, parse complete NDJSON lines, buffer partials.\"\"\"\n        # Prepend any leftover partial line from previous poll\n        data = self._line_buffer + chunk\n        lines = data.split(\"\\n\")\n\n        # Last element is either \"\" (if chunk ended with \\n) or a partial line\n        self._line_buffer = lines[-1]\n\n        # Process all complete lines (everything except the last split element)\n        for line in lines[:-1]:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n\n            # Update liveness on every complete line\n            self.state.last_growth_time = now\n            self.state.last_event_time = now\n            self.state.stall_seconds = 0.0\n            self.state.events_received += 1\n            self.state.lines_total += 1\n\n            # Try to parse as JSON\n            try:\n                event = json.loads(line)\n            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):\n                # Not valid JSON — still counts as a line for liveness,\n                # but try text-mode signal extraction as fallback\n                self._extract_signals_from_text(line)\n                continue\n\n            self._extract_signals_from_event(event)\n\n    def _extract_signals_from_event(self, event: dict):\n        \"\"\"Extract task IDs, tool names, file paths from a parsed NDJSON event.\"\"\"\n        event_type = event.get(\"type\", \"\")\n        debug_log(_dbg, \"ndjson_line_parsed\", event_type=event_type, parsed=True)\n\n        # Tool use events: extract tool name directly\n        if event_type == \"tool_use\":\n            tool = event.get(\"tool\", \"\")\n            if tool:\n                self.state.last_tool_used = tool\n\n        # Stringify the event for regex-based signal extraction\n        # This catches task IDs and file changes regardless of event structure\n        text = json.dumps(event, default=str)\n        self._extract_signals_from_text(text)\n\n    def _extract_signals_from_text(self, text: str):\n        \"\"\"Extract task IDs, tool names, file paths from text using regex.\"\"\"\n        # Last task ID (take the last match)\n        task_matches = TASK_ID_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        if task_matches:\n            self.state.last_task_id = task_matches[-1]\n            debug_log(_dbg, \"signal_extracted\", signal_type=\"task_id\", value=task_matches[-1])\n\n        # Last tool used (only if not already set by structured extraction)\n        tool_matches = TOOL_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        if tool_matches:\n            self.state.last_tool_used = tool_matches[-1]\n            debug_log(_dbg, \"signal_extracted\", signal_type=\"tool_name\", value=tool_matches[-1])\n\n        # Files changed (accumulate unique paths)\n        file_matches = FILES_CHANGED_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        for f in file_matches:\n            self._seen_files.add(f)\n        self.state.files_changed = len(self._seen_files)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":59,"oldLines":6,"newStart":59,"newLines":41,"lines":["     return False"," "," ","+# Pattern for counting assistant message turns in NDJSON output","+# Each \"assistant\" type message represents one turn consumed.","+_TURN_INDICATOR_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\"type\"\\s*:\\s*\"assistant\"')","+","+","+def count_turns_from_output(output_path: Path) -> int:","+    \"\"\"Extract the number of turns consumed from subprocess NDJSON output.","+","+    Counts lines containing ``\"type\":\"assistant\"`` which represent","+    individual assistant response turns. \"result.md\"\n        # CONTINUE appears before HALT — HALT must still win\n        result_file.write_text(\n            \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\\n\"\n            \"Some tasks failed.\\n\"\n            \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\\n\"\n        )\n        output_file = tmp_path / \"output.txt\"\n\n        status = _determine_phase_status(\n            exit_code=0,\n \"\"\"Tests for per-task subprocess orchestration loop.\"\"\"\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _make_tasks(count: int = 3) -> list[TaskEntry]:\n        return [\n            TaskEntry(\n                task_id=f\"T02.{i:02d}\",\n                title=f\"Task {i}\",\n                dependencies=[f\"T02.{i-1:02d}\"] if i > 1 else [],\n            )\n            for i in range(1, count + 1)\n        ]\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _pass_factory(task, config, phase):\n        \"\"\"Subprocess factory that always succeeds with 3 turns consumed.\"\"\"\n        return (0, 3, 1024)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _fail_factory(task, config, phase):\n        \"\"\"Subprocess factory that always fails.\"\"\"\n        return (1, 5, 512)\n\n    def test_per_task_spawns_one_subprocess_per_task(self, tmp_path):\n        config = _make_config(tmp_path, num_phases=1)\n        phase = config.phases[0]\n        tasks = self._make_tasks(3)\n\n        spawn_count = [0]\n\n        def counting_factory(task, config, phase):\n            spawn_count[0] += 1\n            return (0, 2, 100)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, _subprocess_factory=counting_factory\n        )\n        assert spawn_count[0] == 3\n        assert len(results) == 3\n        assert remaining == []\n\n    def TurnLedger(initial_budget=8, minimum_allocation=5)\n\n        def consume_factory(task, config, phase):\n            return (0, 5, 100)\n\n        results, remaining = execute_phase_tasks(\n            tasks, config, phase, ledger=ledger,\n            _subprocess_factory=consume_factory,\n        )\n        # First task succeeds, next two should be skipped due to budget\n        passed = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.PASS]\n        skipped = [r for r in results if r.status == TaskStatus.SKIPPED]\n        assert len(passed) == 1\n        assert len(skipped) == 2\n        assert
